title: Cleanup, Partial Initialization, and Error Paths · Memory Trace: Error Cause Preservation
summary: A configuration loader acquires buffers, files, locks, and parsed objects before failures at each stage. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cmem error cause preservation increment in failure-atomic resource constructor. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Preserve the first causal error while still recording cleanup failures and resource state.
Build replacement state separately and commit only after validation so callers retain a usable old value on failure.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Error Cause Preservation · Theory Error Cause Preservation
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory native-systems team handles memory case 9402. Produce bounded C evidence to unwind partially acquired resources in reverse order; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Preserve the first causal error while still recording cleanup failures and resource state.
